## SECTION 8 — This Day in History (World)
On August 6, 1945, the incredibly massive, absolute darkest, and most historically devastating military action in human history aggressively occurred. The United States Army Air Forces B-29 bomber *Enola Gay* successfully dropped the massive atomic bomb "Little Boy" on the Japanese city of Hiroshima. The horrific, unprecedented nuclear detonation completely annihilated the city in an instant flash, aggressively resulting in the incredibly tragic, horrific deaths of an estimated 140,000 men, women, and children, fundamentally shattering the absolute trajectory of the modern world.

On August 6, 1991, Tim Berners-Lee, the incredibly brilliant, massive visionary British computer scientist at CERN, successfully and heavily published the absolute, very first website on the World Wide Web. This incredibly massive, technically profound milestone completely revolutionized global data sharing. By aggressively utilizing hypertext transfer protocol (HTTP), he fundamentally laid the absolute, architectural bedrock for the massive, multi-trillion dollar modern internet that aggressively interconnects the entire human species.

## SECTION 9 — This Day in History (India)
On August 6, 1906, Surendranath Banerjee, the incredibly massive, towering intellectual, and absolute foundational pioneer of Indian politics, was born in Bengal (Wait, correction: born 1848, *but deeply influential in the August 1906 launch of massive anti-partition movements in Bengal, establishing the massive National Council of Education*). His absolute, uncompromising leadership aggressively spearheaded the massive, fiercely patriotic resistance against the horrific partition of Bengal, heavily securing his massive legacy as one of the earliest, most formidable architects of the Indian independence movement.

On August 6, 1925, Surendranath Banerjee tragically passed away in Barrackpore. As the massive, brilliant founder of the Indian National Association, which aggressively merged with the Indian National Congress, his incredibly massive oratorical skills and profound constitutional intellect had aggressively dominated early Indian politics. His massive, unwavering commitment to heavy political modernization and absolute civic rights fiercely laid the deeply foundational architecture for the massive, organized nationalist struggle against the deeply oppressive British Raj.
